explanation: The response correctly identifies register-spill as the bottleneck with HIGH confidence, cites specific metric values (255 registers, 106.6% DRAM BW, 91.5% scoreboard stalls, 15.4% occupancy) and concrete code evidence (local array sizes totaling 576 floats), and correctly frames DRAM saturation as a symptom of local memory spill traffic rather than the primary cause.
